issue.title: Toast action buttons (Retry / Open Settings / etc.) (E68)

phases.title: Toast API + render: optional action button + framework-outdated proof-of-concept consumer
verification: `toastStore.ts` exports a `ToastAction` interface; the `Toast` interface gains optional `action?: ToastAction`; `addToast` signature accepts a 4th optional `action?: ToastAction` parameter.
All ~12 existing `addToast(message, type, duration)` call sites compile and run unchanged (no 4th arg required).

`Toast.tsx` renders a `<button className="toast-action" type="button">` between the message and the dismiss control when `toast.action` is present; the button's onClick runs the action in a try/catch and then calls `onClose()` in a finally so the toast always dismisses.

`Toast.css` gains a `.toast-action` rule with `flex-shrink: 0`, neutral neutral-bordered styling using `--text-secondary`, `--border-color`, `--bg-hover`, and a `:focus-visible` ring using `--accent-color`.

One real consumer is wired up — either: (a) the framework-outdated warning in `ProjectsSection.tsx` with a custom-event-based 'Update' action that triggers the existing `handleInstallFramework` flow in `ProjectCard.tsx`; or (b) the issue-failed error toast in `App.tsx:69` with a 'View Issue' action calling `setSelectedIssue`. Document which was chosen in the phase summary.
Tab order within a toast carrying both an action and a dismiss control: action focuses first, dismiss second.
Toasts without an `action` field render identically to today (no visual regression, no extra DOM).
`pnpm build` passes (tsc -b strict mode + vite); `pnpm test` is green (89 desktop tests preserved).
Manual smoke verified per the test plan.
phases.summary: Added `ToastAction` interface and optional `action?: ToastAction` 4th-arg to `addToast` in toastStore.ts; rendered a conditional `.toast-action` button between message and dismiss in Toast.tsx with try/catch+finally guaranteeing dismiss; added matching neutral-bordered CSS in Toast.css. Wired consumer **option (a)**: framework-outdated warning in ProjectsSection.tsx now carries an 'Update' action that dispatches `tiki:install-framework` (with projectPath), and ProjectCard.tsx subscribes via a `useEffect` listener that calls the existing install flow when its project matches. `pnpm build` clean (tsc -b + vite); `pnpm test` green (shared 65/65, desktop vitest 89/89 preserved, Rust 21+7/28).
